req.title: Allergy intolerance (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient; subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-084 mandates three verifiable behaviours: (1) a clinician can POST a valid AllergyIntolerance and receive 201 + an AuditEvent (positive/EP); (2) posting the exact same allergy a second time returns 409 (negative/NEG); (3) the system handles the state boundary where an allergy is created then a conflicting MedicationRequest is submitted, triggering a warning alert (boundary/STC). Each test is self-contained via pytest fixtures that create a fresh patient, authenticate as clinician, and clean up via teardown. Determinism is enforced by using unique patient identifiers per test run (uuid4).

tests.name: test_post_allergy_intolerance_creates_resource_and_audit_event
preconditions: A patient resource exists with known patient_id
No prior AllergyIntolerance for this patient+substance combination
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with a valid FHIR R4 payload referencing the patient and substance 'peanut'
Assert HTTP 201 and capture resource id from Location header
expected: AuditEvent query returns at least one event with action='C' and outcome='0'
trace: R-HLT-084: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from <helper_fn> import fhir_client, clinician_token, create_patient, delete_resource

@pytest.fixture()
def ctx():
    token = clinician_token()
    patient_id = create_patient(token)
    yield {"token": token, "patient_id": patient_id}
    delete_resource(token, f"Patient/{patient_id}")

def test_post_allergy_intolerance_creates_resource_and_audit_event(ctx):
    client = fhir_client(ctx["token"])
    substance_code = "762952008"
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"code": "active"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{ctx['patient_id']}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct", "code": substance_code}]}
    }
    post_resp = client.post("/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201, post_resp.text
    resource_id = post_resp.json()["id"]
    assert post_resp.json()["resourceType"] == "AllergyIntolerance"
    audit_resp = client.get(f"/AuditEvent?entity=AllergyIntolerance/{resource_id}")
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1
    event = entries[0]["resource"]
    assert event["action"] == "C"
    assert event["outcome"] == "0"
    delete_resource(ctx["token"], f"AllergyIntolerance/{resource_id}")

tests.name: test_post_duplicate_allergy_intolerance_returns_409
preconditions: A patient resource exists with known patient_id
An AllergyIntolerance for the same patient+substance already exists
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with a valid payload (first insert — expect 201)
POST /AllergyIntolerance with the identical payload a second time
Assert HTTP 409 on the second POST
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome with issue.code='duplicate'
trace: R-HLT-084: error pathway — 409 when exact duplicate
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import fhir_client, clinician_token, create_patient, delete_resource

@pytest.fixture()
def ctx():
    token = clinician_token()
    patient_id = create_patient(token)
    yield {"token": token, "patient_id": patient_id}
    delete_resource(token, f"Patient/{patient_id}")

def test_post_duplicate_allergy_intolerance_returns_409(ctx):
    client = fhir_client(ctx["token"])
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"code": "active"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{ctx['patient_id']}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct", "code": "372687004"}]}
    }
    first = client.post("/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload)
    assert first.status_code == 201, first.text
    resource_id = first.json()["id"]
    second = client.post("/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload)
    assert second.status_code == 409, second.text
    body = second.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "OperationOutcome"
    codes = [i.get("code") for i in body.get("issue", [])]
    assert "duplicate" in codes
    delete_resource(ctx["token"], f"AllergyIntolerance/{resource_id}")

tests.name: test_medication_request_warns_on_allergy_conflict_after_allergy_recorded
preconditions: A patient resource exists with known patient_id
No prior AllergyIntolerance or MedicationRequest for this patient
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance for substance 'penicillin' — expect 201 (state: allergy stored)
POST /MedicationRequest prescribing a medication containing penicillin for the same patient
Assert response contains a conflict warning
Verify AuditEvent emitted for both operations
expected: MedicationRequest POST returns HTTP 201 or 422 with a detectedIssue of type 'drug-allergy-alert'
AuditEvent records exist for both the AllergyIntolerance and the MedicationRequest
trace: R-HLT-084: subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict
R-HLT-084: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
